title: Molten Dutch Brussels Sprouts Casserole
description: A resinous, molten Dutch favorite featuring caramelized Brussels sprouts topped with a rich confit-tomato and cream sauce, low-carb and crowd-pleasing.
instructions: Trim the ends of the Brussels sprouts, cut them in half, and rinse them well.
Toss the sprouts with olive oil, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper until evenly coated.
Spread the seasoned sprouts on a baking sheet and roast for 20 minutes until caramelized and slightly browned.
While the sprouts roast, heat a small saucepan over medium heat and add the confit tomato, simmering for 5 minutes to thicken the sauce.
Remove the saucepan from heat and stir in the heavy cream and grated Parmesan until the mixture becomes a thick, molten sauce.
Transfer the roasted sprouts to a baking dish and pour the molten sauce over them, spreading evenly.
Sprinkle bacon bits over the top for an optional smoky finish.
Return the casserole to the oven and bake for an additional 10 minutes until the sauce bubbles and the top is golden.
Serve hot directly from the oven for a rich, molten experience.
